title: MILITARY ONESOURCE - 211info
text: MILITARY ONESOURCE MILITARY ONESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides phone and internet support service through the Department of Defense, offering assistance and resources to military personnel and their families on various issues. Services include counseling and information on personal issues, money matters, career issues, parenting, legal issues, elder care, relocation, violence, trauma, and transition to civilian life. Website Eligibility: Individuals who meet one or more of the following criteria: -Active duty service member of the Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, or Space Force and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Service members of the National Guard and reserve and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Coast Guard members who are activated as part of the Department of the Navy under Title 10 authority, as well as their immediate family members -Survivors who have not remarried and children of active-duty, National Guard, and reserve service members (regardless of activation status or cause of service member's death) -Medically discharged service members and their immediate family members currently enrolled in their service branch's wounded warrior or seriously ill and injured program -Retired service members and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date, including service members on the Temporary Disability Retirement List. Coast Guard veterans and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service -Discharged service members (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date. Coast Guard veterans (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service. -Designated Department of Defense (DOD) expeditionary civilians, as defined by Directive-type Memorandum (DTM)-17-004, “Department of Defense Expeditionary Civilian Workforce” during the 90 days prior to deployment and 180 days post-deployment. -Immediate family of designated DOD expeditionary civilians, including spouses, children and anyone who has legal responsibility for workforce member’s children, for the benefit of the children -Military academy cadets, students enrolled in one of the service academies for the Army, Navy or Air Force -Active-duty foreign military service members assigned to U.S. Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force and Space Force installations who are registered in DEERS. Foreign military service members who have DOD-issued CAC with MWR/commissary/exchange privileges. -Immediate family members of foreign military active-duty service members registered in Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System (DEERS). This includes spouse as defined by 10 USC Section 101 and children. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 342-9647 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (800) 342-9647 Text Short Code 838255 Veterans / Military Crisis Line INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies by service LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Nationwide Services: Military Transition Assistance Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Veteran Reintegration Counseling Veteran/Military Hotlines Wellness Programs * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
